studio.name: B.I.A.V. Studio
studio.previous_project: My Turn (逆转回合) — fair-play card battle game; design thinking carried into Morimens card mechanics and Traphase mode
studio.art_team_note: Most were fresh graduates recruited by lead artist; established the visual style; majority left around late 2024 First Arc finale; new art team now taking over with flexible collaboration with former artists

timeline.2019: Project founded; three core pillars established: roguelike card mechanics, Cthulhu themes, anime art style
timeline.2020: First concept PV released, attracted more developers
timeline.2021_end: Development bottleneck + cost pressure + unclear core gameplay + Genshin Impact raising market expectations → near-dissolution. Light joins as second producer, restructures core gameplay, drops high-cost exploration and overly complex fragmented narrative, refocuses from investigation to combat. Game officially named 'Morimens' during this restart
timeline.2023_h2: Ported to PC and launched on Steam ahead of potential shutdown. Earned 'Very Positive' rating despite gacha skepticism from Steam community
timeline.2024_end: Second crisis; core members left; First Arc finale released
timeline.2025_2026: Team rebuilding; Steam ~1000 DAU; Discord 37,533 members

narrative.original_plan: 3 arcs, each ~10 chapters of main story

arc1.compression_details: Ch7-8 were supposed to be Keeper's encounter with Tinct and Faint at opera house. Current Ch7 contains content from original Ch9 (Thais's arc). Current Ch8 contains content from original Arc 3 Ch10. Church of the Lightbearers and Sculptors' Association content never fully developed
arc1.ch8_note: Primarily functions as exposition — establishes core lore, organizes threads, delivers provisional character resolutions. Writer faced unprecedented challenges

arc2.title: Astral Reign / When the Stars Are Right (星辰正位之刻)
arc2.exploration_removed: Removed due to high co-design difficulty, low reusability, limited meaningful branching. May return selectively at key narrative moments
arc2.key_reveal: The entity Tulu serves will be one of the key figures in Arc 2's concluding stages

arc3.status: Early discussions on plot outline and character directions
arc3.will_resolve: Many previously undisclosed world-building details and hanging narrative threads

primary_lovecraft: At the Mountains of Madness (first inspiration for the project)
Through the Gates of the Silver Key
Ex Oblivione (one of the true souls of this project)
literature: Thomas Ligotti's The Frolic (homaged in a stage title)
game_inspirations: Chaos Zero Nightmare (card variation during exploration)

characters.unrevealed: A mysterious Ultra Realm Genesis character — concept finalized but not yet revealed
characters.doll_inferno: Originally a central figure for Arc 3; had a standalone personal storyline (initial draft complete) that was never released due to Arc 1 compression
characters.lights_favorite: Ramona: Timeworn — personally designed her skill set; her ability to replicate last card played mirrors 'drawing strength from past experiences'; added line to V1.1 PV ending: 'No matter how many times I fail, I will ultimately save you'
characters.24_design: True exception — compresses four character niches into one unit. Not commercially cost-effective but done to serve 'split personality' narrative. Battle designers 'burned once for their ideals'
characters.most_difficult_to_write_recently: Murphy: Fauxborn — original Murphy's story was already complete, limited expansion room; solution: portrayed as Divine Mother facade hiding pure inner self

three_core_principles: 1. Unify gameplay and narrative — cards express character identity, not just numbers
2. Make every choice meaningful — every team comp, relic, card order should create distinct strategic trade-offs
3. Buffs only, no nerfs (as much as possible) — gacha is a service commitment; if nerf unavoidable, provide refund mechanism

cut_mechanic.name: Madness Scale (疯狂量表)
cut_mechanic.concept: Would shift based on exploration choices, granting dangerous but powerful abilities in final phase
why_cut: Cognitive burden too high with existing systems
Stage length too variable for granularity
Character identity mismatch — Keeper chooses to face the indescribable, shouldn't be frequently broken

game_design.original_codename_domain_vision: Narrative focused on campus life, repeated Domain exploration, gradual truth discovery; detailed Mythag University environments; Awakeners only determine starting cards which transform into variants during battle; realm-universal cards collectible during exploration
game_design.hardest_to_design: Integrating narrative, exploration, and combat into coherent experience without losing player goals/motivation

confirmed: Standard Awakener 'Pure Core' progression to bridge gap with Limited Awakeners (coming very soon)
Long-form roguelike deckbuilder mode (Train was demo-level test, direction validated)
Music gameplay mode (inspired by Deemo; Frank has composed hundreds of tracks)
Customizable login screen (officially added to schedule during this interview)
Reopening expired Echo'd Pilgrimage packages at reasonable intervals
Increasing free benefits and event rewards with every major version
under_consideration: Guild cooperative gameplay (2-player co-op prioritized over large raids)
Selective reintroduction of exploration at key narrative moments
Madness Mode for story missions (delayed — Hard Mode completion rate below expectations)
not_planned: More multi-realm Awakeners like 24 (unless narrative demands it)
New non-gacha Awakeners or welfare units with special acquisition
Major progression system additions (unless game reaches a stage needing new long-term goals)

collaboration.upcoming: One-way collaboration with a classic work of profound influence with cosmic horror flavor. Special character crossover
collaboration.policy: No one-time content; all collab content will be obtainable again. Licensing typically multi-year with unlimited reruns

monetization_philosophy.gacha_stance: Not defending the model itself; explaining specific choices within it
player_friendly_measures: Monthly exchangeable universal special materials (Pure Cores)
Removed Normal Mode emergency gnosis restriction
Almost no truly permanently unavailable core content
Refund mechanism when nerfs are unavoidable
admitted_mistakes: Enlighten/Wheel duplicate requirements set too high initially
Over-Exaltation, extra level caps, Final Axiom added later created psychological burden
First year nerfs caused poor experience; later reverted some
Too casual community communication in year 1 led to amplified criticism during monetization adjustments
monetization_philosophy.future_direction: Increase free benefits per major version; more chances to obtain previously limited content; convenience and stress-reducing features; optimize duplicate overflow rewards

offline_version_plan.commitment: If servers ever shut down, provide offline-playable version
offline_version_plan.technical_approach: Embed 'virtual private server' locally to simulate server logic
offline_version_plan.scope: Preserves progress, progression, cosmetics, achievements, gallery; open to new players too (not just commemorative)
offline_version_plan.current_action: Maintaining architecture separability during development; budget reserved
offline_version_plan.limitations: No cross-platform sync in offline version; gacha/stamina settings won't change significantly to respect historical investment
offline_version_plan.legal_status: Not all legal and technical details finalized yet

community_engagement.thpdom_impact: THPDom's video 'Why I fell in love with this card-based gacha game' moved Light to tears; directly led to voiceover budget for Final Chapter's last section; brought many new loyal players
community_engagement.content_creator_sponsorship: Exploring suitable and sustainable collaboration methods
community_engagement.fan_events: Irregular fan creation events on Discord; more planned
community_engagement.lights_hardest_lesson: Too casual year-1 communication + exhaustion → poor handling of criticism → considered resigning → realized best redemption is making the game better
